skill.name: audit
skill.description: Perform comprehensive audit of interface quality across accessibility, performance, theming, and responsive design. Generates detailed report of issues with severity ratings and recommendations.
skill.summary: Audit frontend interface quality across accessibility, performance, theming, and responsive design.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and appear to be false positives. The command-like strings at lines 114-116 are example slash commands for user guidance, not executable shell instructions, and no weak cryptographic algorithm use was found. No prompt injection, network access, filesystem access, secret handling, or malicious intent was identified.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Example Slash Commands
low_findings.description: SKILL.md lines 114-116 mention slash commands such as /normalize, /optimize, and /harden as examples for a generated audit report. They are plain Markdown guidance, not shell backticks, subprocess calls, or instructions to execute arbitrary commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown bullet examples and contain no shell syntax or executable code. The surrounding context asks the model to suggest available commands, not run them.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptographic algorithm detections point to prose in SKILL.md, including audit categories and severity labels. No cryptographic API, hash function, cipher configuration, credential handling, or encoded payload appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found only natural-language audit instructions at the cited locations. There is no code path where cryptographic behavior could be invoked.

content.value_statement: Frontend teams need clear evidence before improving visual quality, accessibility, and performance. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to produce prioritized interface audit reports.
actual_capabilities: Reviews accessibility risks such as contrast, labels, keyboard flow, semantic structure, and form feedback.
Checks performance concerns including layout thrashing, expensive animations, unoptimized assets, and unnecessary renders.
Evaluates theming issues such as hard-coded colors, dark mode gaps, inconsistent tokens, and theme switching failures.
Assesses responsive behavior, touch target size, horizontal overflow, text scaling, and missing breakpoints.
Produces a structured report with severity, impact, standards, recommendations, and suggested follow-up commands.
Identifies recurring patterns and positive findings so teams can prioritize systemic improvements.
limitations: It documents issues but explicitly does not fix code.
It depends on available project context and installed skills for command suggestions.
It may require manual verification for visual judgment, browser behavior, or WCAG interpretation.
It is focused on frontend interface quality, not backend security or infrastructure review.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Release Quality Review
use_cases.description: Audit a feature before launch and receive a prioritized list of blockers, high-impact defects, and follow-up actions.

use_cases.title: Standardize Design System Adoption
use_cases.description: Find hard-coded colors, inconsistent tokens, broken themes, and repeated layout patterns across interface components.

use_cases.title: Document Accessibility Debt
use_cases.description: Create a clear report of accessibility risks with user impact, likely standards, and remediation priorities.

prompt_templates.title: Audit One Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the audit skill to review the current screen for accessibility, responsive design, theming, and performance issues. Do not fix the code.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a focused interface area.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Feature Area
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the checkout flow and produce a severity-ranked report with locations, impact, standards, recommendations, and suggested follow-up commands.

prompt_templates.title: Find Systemic UI Problems
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the dashboard for recurring accessibility, performance, theming, and responsive design patterns. Include positive findings and priority recommendations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Team review of repeated interface quality problems.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Remediation Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a comprehensive audit for the account settings area. Group issues by severity, identify systemic causes, and map each major issue to the best available follow-up command.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a cleanup sprint.

output_examples.output: A severity-ranked report listing touch target issues, keyboard focus gaps, overflow risks, and prioritized recommendations.

output_examples.input: Review the dashboard for design system consistency.
output_examples.output: A theming report identifying hard-coded colors, token mismatches, dark mode defects, positive examples, and next-step priorities.

output_examples.input: Audit the product detail page before release.
output_examples.output: An executive summary with top issues, detailed findings by severity, systemic patterns, positive findings, and suggested commands.

best_practices: Run the audit on a specific feature or screen when you need precise locations.
Provide available commands so the report can recommend valid follow-up actions.
Verify visual and accessibility findings in a browser before scheduling remediation work.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as a replacement for automated accessibility testing.
Do not ask it to fix issues during the audit pass.
Do not treat every low-severity note as release-blocking work.

faq.question: Does this skill change files?
faq.answer: No. The instructions explicitly tell the assistant to document issues and avoid fixing code.

faq.question: What areas does it audit?
faq.answer: It covers accessibility, performance, theming, responsive design, anti-patterns, systemic issues, and positive findings.

faq.question: Can it suggest commands for fixes?
faq.answer: Yes. It can map findings to available commands, but it should not invent commands that are not installed.

faq.question: Is it only for web interfaces?
faq.answer: It is best suited for frontend web interfaces and design system work.

faq.question: Does it replace expert accessibility review?
faq.answer: No. It helps organize findings, but manual testing and specialist review may still be required.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
